judgeReasoning: Information is complete and chronologically organized, but the agent would need to parse 10 distinct topics to orient. Active work is identifiable (poems in queue, memory compaction done, budget constraints active, Discord/Puppeteer next) but buried among completed items. The '[topic: X]' structure helps but doesn't prioritize—what should the agent focus on NOW? A 'Current priorities' section would make this immediately actionable instead of requiring the agent to reconstruct context from narrative.
